properties.Initiative_Type: Direct Access, Education and Culture, Production & Harvest, Knowledge & Infrastructure Development
properties.Affiliation: Chisasibi Eeyou Resource and Research Institute (CERRI)
properties.Title: Geodesic Greenhouse Dome
properties.Description: The 33-foot diameter geodesic dome greenhouse built in 2023 enhances agricultural capabilities, supports CERRI's mission of food sovereignty, and serves as a model for sustainable building practices. It will run all year round and produce fresh greens and vegetables. The greenhouse also includes a biodigester and a worm composter.
properties.Address: Behind the CERRI Office, 15 Mamuu Rd, Chisasibi, QC, Canada, J0M 1E0

properties.Affiliation: Chisasibi Eeyou Resource and Research Institute (CERRI)
properties.Title: Second Greenhouse and Hydroponics
properties.Description: CERRI has built an additional 1200 sqft greenhouse that will start operations in the summer of 2026. CERRI will also install a hydroponics container farm, whereby two containers of space will be dedicated to growing lettuce and strawberries.
properties.Address: Behind the CERRI Office, 15 Mamuu Rd, Chisasibi, QC, Canada, J0M 1E0

properties.Affiliation: Chisasibi Eeyou Resource and Research Institute (CERRI)
properties.Title: Chisasibi Farm
properties.Description: CERRI has acquired some farm equipment, tractors, etc., to start farming a roughly 4-acre parcel of land.  In summer 2025, they tilled the land, in summer 2026 they will grow a cover crop, which can bring nutrients to the soil in order to grow an edible crop in summer 2027.
properties.Address: Behind the CERRI Office, 15 Mamuu Rd, Chisasibi, QC, Canada, J0M 1E0

properties.Initiative_Type: Direct Access, Education and Culture, Production & Harvest, Knowledge & Infrastructure Development
properties.Title: School Greenhouse
properties.Description: The Big River High School relocated to a new building in fall 2024 with a brand new rooftop greenhouse that will serve educational purposes and provide fresh produce to the cafeteria, such as bell peppers, herbs, greens, sweetgrass and potatoes. The greenhouse is co-managed by the high school and the Chisasibi Eeyou Resource and Research Institute.
properties.Address: 91 Aahtisskuu Rd, Chisasibi, QC, Canada, J0M 1E0

properties.Title: Emergency Food Baskets
properties.Description: Emergency food baskets provide a three-day food supply for pregnant and post-partum women, offered twice per year at the CMC during emergencies.
properties.Address: 12 Maamuu, Chisasibi, QC, Canada, J0M 1E0

properties.Title: ᒥᔫᐊᔑᒥᔓᐦ Miyuu-Ashimishuh
properties.Description: The Miyû-Ashimishuh program offers weekly coupons to pregnant mothers for healthy food until two months post-delivery. Participants can get weekly grocery coupons for nutritious foods such as milk, eggs, fruits and vegetables.
properties.Contact: Contact your local Awash nurse, PCCR, or nutritionist.
properties.Address: 12 Maamuu, Chisasibi, QC, Canada, J0M 1E0
properties.Links:
Awash Services Website:
https://www.creehealth.org/services/awash-services-children-0-9 ;
Pregnancy Healthy Eating Website:
https://www.creehealth.org/health-tips/pregnancy-healthy-eating

properties.Title: Nutritional Counselling
properties.Description: Nutrition counselling can take place in an individual session or in a group/workshop setting (such as a cooking or baby-food-making class). To meet with a nutritionist, get an appointment at the CMC with a nurse or doctor, and discuss your nutritional needs with them. You can also call the MSDC and ask to speak with the nutritionist there.
properties.Address: 12 Maamuu, Chisasibi, QC, Canada, J0M 1E0

properties.Affiliation: Cree Health Board (CBHSSJB), Cree Nation of Chisasibi
properties.Title: Chisasibi Food Pantry
properties.Description: The Cree Health Board is partnering with the Banques Alimentaires du Québec to provide a monthly food bank. Starting in September 2025, bags of goods will be provided to families in need. The remaining food will be made available to the whole community. Eligibility is assessed by the psychosocial team (CHB), and the food pantry is located at the CMC.
properties.Contact: Anita Napash and Nellie Bobbish (CNC), Hepzibeth Lee (CHB)

properties.Affiliation: Cree Nation of Chisasibi, Youth Council / Youth Department
properties.Title: Gift from the Heart
properties.Description: Run by the Chisasibi Youth Council, the Gift from the Heart program helps eligible youth in need receive a Christmas gift by anonymously matching them with a generous community member. The gift also includes turkey and food to help relieve pressure during the holiday season.
properties.Address: 14-B, Maamuu Road, Chisasibi, QC, Canada, J0M 1E0

properties.Affiliation: Cree Nation of Chisasibi, Youth Council / Youth Department
properties.Title: Youth Center Snack Program
properties.Description: Every day, the youth centre provides healthy snacks to the youth attending the centre.  Every few weeks, the snacks are paired with educational activities on healthy eating in collaboration with the Cree Health Board Nutritionist or led by the youth centre staff.
properties.Contact: Corinna Napash, Program Coordinator for the Youth Centre

properties.Title: Breakfast Club Program
properties.Description: Elementary & high school students are provided breakfast through a partnership between Breakfast Club of Canada and regional entities (Cree School Board, Cree Nation Government, the Cree Health Board).
properties.Contact:
École Upichinaausiun Chiskutimatukimikw School, Elementary – Pre-K to Grade 2
Waapinichikush Elementary School, Elementary – Grade 3 to 6 
Big River High School
properties.Address: 36 Industrial Road, Chisasibi, QC, Canada, J0M 1E0

properties.Affiliation: Chisasibi Eeyou Resource and Research Institute (CERRI)
properties.Title: Eelgrass research and Traditional ecological knowledge
properties.Description: This research project uses Traditional Ecological Knowledge and environmental science to examine the extent and mechanisms behind the reduction in Eelgrass meadows (Zostera marina) in James Bay at the mouth of the La Grande River since the 1970s. These are fundamental ecosystems for the Cree families of Chisasibi because of their capacity to attract waterfowl species such as Canada Geese, Brant Geese, Snow Geese, and various duck species.
properties.Contact: Dante Torio, Lead Scientist and Marine Biologist

properties.Title: Service of Traditional Food in Cree Health Board Food Services
properties.Description: Traditional food may be served in the Cree Health Board food services (elders' homes, MSDCs, Hospital), when available. Traditional food is provided by Cree Hunters who received training on the CHB traditional food program, including food safety and hygiene.
properties.Contact: Lilian Kandiliotis and Chantal Vinet-Lanouette, Public Health Nutritionists
properties.Address: 12 Maamuu, Chisasibi, QC, Canada, J0M 1E0

properties.Title: Economic Security Program for Cree Hunters
properties.Description: The Economic Security Program for Cree Hunters is a family program which provides an annual income to Crees who have chosen hunting, trapping and fishing activities as a way of life. The program encourages the Crees to pursue harvesting activities by providing them with an income guaranteed, benefits and other incentives.
properties.Contact: Local Administrators: Sally Herodier, Natalie Scipio, Angelina Ratt-Louttit
properties.Address: 13, Maamuu Road, Chisasibi, QC, Canada, J0M 1E0
properties.Links: Cree Hunter Economic Security Board Website: https://www.chasseurcri.ca/about-us

properties.Initiative_Type: Access to the Land, Education and Culture
properties.Affiliation: Cree Nation Government's Justice and Correctional Services
properties.Title: Community Justice Committees Food and Land Related Programming
properties.Description: The Community Justice Committees are responsible for much of the land-based programming offered in the Nation, with people who are at risk or have come into contact with the justice system. Initiatives vary per year and community, but can include preparing caribou hides, caribou hunting, and allowing youth and elders to connect while on the land.
properties.Address: 461 Wolverine Road, QC, Canada, J0M 1E0

properties.Title: Musuutaau Blueberry Festival
properties.Description: The Yearly Blueberry Festival happens at the end of summer and is composed of a blueberry picking day and a blueberry baking contest. Many chose to donate part of their harvest to the multi-service day centre and the Community Miyupimaatisiiun Centre.

properties.Affiliation: Cree Nation of Chisasibi, Elders Department
properties.Title: Meals on Wheels
properties.Description: This program runs on and off depending on the availability of traditional foods. It provides elders with traditional food and some store-bought foods. particularly elders whose families cannot go out on the land to get traditional foods themselves. Funded by the CNG, this initiative is run by the Elders' Council under the band's Department of Cultural and Social Development.

properties.Affiliation: Cree Nation of Chisasibi, Youth Council / Youth Department
properties.Title: Adopt a Youth Goose Break Program
properties.Description: Run by the Chisasibi Youth Council, the Adopt a Youth Goose Break Program provides grocery vouchers and travel expense reimbursement for participants to join other families during their goose break outings to their traplines. This initiative runs every two years.
properties.Address: 14-B, Maamuu Road, Chisasibi, QC, Canada, J0M 1E0

properties.Affiliation: Cree Nation of Chisasibi, Youth Council / Youth Department
properties.Title: Young Families and Youth Goose Break Programs
properties.Description: Run by the Chisasibi Youth Council, these programs provide grocery vouchers to youth families and youth during goose break.
properties.Address: 14-B, Maamuu Road, Chisasibi, QC, Canada, J0M 1E0

properties.Initiative_Type: Access to the Land, Education and Culture
properties.Affiliation: Cree Nation of Chisasibi, Youth Council / Youth Department
properties.Title: Moose Break Trip
properties.Description: The Chisasibi Youth Council organizes a hunting trip to bring youth out on the land, accompanied by a guide and elders. They learn how to cut and clean their kill, chop wood and cook food in the traditional ways.
properties.Address: 14-B, Maamuu Road, Chisasibi, QC, Canada, J0M 1E0

properties.Title: COTA's Sustainable Non-Timber Forest Products (NTFP) Project
properties.Description: Initiated in 2019 by the Cree Outfitting and Tourism Association (COTA), the project aims to improve the quality of the visitor experience in Eeyou Istchee by integrating non-timber forest products (NTFPs), mainly edible mushrooms and berries, into local restaurants and tourism offerings. A study was conducted to understand the distribution and abundance of NTFPs in collaboration with FaunENord. Areas surveyed include the Billy Diamond Highway, along some of the access roads, around Waswanipi and Mistissini, and on several islands near the communities of Waskaganish, Eastmain, Wemindji, and Chisasibi.
properties.Contact:
Rob Imrie, Director of Development, Robin McGinley Executive Director, and Emmanuelle Pelletier
Project Manager, FaunENord

properties.Initiative_Type: Access to the Land, Knowledge & Infrastructure Development
properties.Title: Cabin Reconstruction Initiative
properties.Description: The 2023 Forest fires burnt 11% of Eeyou Istchee and affected nearly 60% of traplines, damaging 142 cabins. The Cree Trappers Association is overseeing funds to rebuild cabins, with 50 being built so far. This project will continue in 2026.
properties.Address: Chisasibi, QC, Canada, J0M 1E0

properties.Title: Access to Traditional Food Program
properties.Description: Local CTA administrators hire local harvesters to provide traditional foods for elders, single-parent and low-income families who may not have access to a hunter or have limited financial resources for harvesting. It focuses mainly on harvesting fish, small game, and waterfowl. This initiative has been in place since the COVID-19 pandemic.
properties.Address: 1 Riverside Drive, Chisasibi, QC, Canada, J0M 1E0
properties.Links:
Chisasibi CTA Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/groups/394741384275542/ 
Traditional Food Program Webpage: https://creetrappers.ca/special-project/traditional-food-program

properties.Title: Community Freezer
properties.Description: The freezer is installed in the CTA warehouse to preserve and share traditional food with community members. Foods will vary according to seasonal availability, and may include moose, caribou, bear, beaver, goose, ptarmigan, fish, and berries.
properties.Address:
PO Box 150
Chisasibi, QC  J0M 1E0

properties.Title: Fort George Mamoweedow
properties.Description: Every summer, the people of Chisasibi gather on the island of Fort George, where the community was originally located. This festival celebrates the culture, language and history of the community, while many traditional activities are organized and traditional food is served.

properties.Title: Eastmain Wellness Food Bank
properties.Description: This initiative is aimed at aiding those who may struggle to put food on their table. Community members who are unemployed, receiving government assistance, living on limited incomes, and those who are struggling to afford food are invited to register with the Public Health Department.
properties.Address:
Eastmain River Complex
Opinica Eastmain, QC, Canada J0M 1W0

properties.Affiliation: Cree Nation of Eastmain, Wellness Centre
properties.Title: Christmas Food Basket
properties.Description: The holiday season can be especially stressful and financially difficult. The Christmas baskets may be the oldest and best-known services to support individuals and families in need. Families in need are invited to apply to The Cree Nation of Eastmain Wellness Center. All registrations are kept confidential.
properties.Address:
Eastmain River Complex
Opinica Eastmain, QC, Canada J0M 1W0

properties.Affiliation: Cree Nation of Eastmain, Wellness Centre
properties.Title: Fruits and Vegetables Program
properties.Description: This initiative is open to all community members and aims to enhance access to nutritious foods. Under this program, participants receive a monthly subsidy on fruit and vegetable orders, with the Public Health Department covering 50% of the total cost. The program focused on promoting healthier lifestyles.
properties.Address:
Eastmain River Complex
Opinica Eastmain, QC, Canada J0M 1W0

properties.Affiliation: Cree Nation of Eastmain, Wellness Centre
properties.Title: Cooking Workshops
properties.Description: The Healthy Cooking aims to teach participants how to cook using a simple approach, to make healthy food with available ingredients, and prepare plant-forward meals. The program also focuses on building cooking skills and nutrition knowledge for at-risk populations and increasing fruit and vegetable intake.
properties.Address:
Eastmain River Complex
Opinica Eastmain, QC, Canada J0M 1W0

properties.Initiative_Type: Access to the Land, Education and Culture
properties.Affiliation: Cree Nation of Nemaska, Social Development Department
properties.Title: Old Nemaska Gathering
properties.Description: This now annual summer event honouring our heritage is one of the highlights of our year. During this event, we promote cultural teaching and host various recreational activities for all ages. Each year, we have a growing population taking part in the gathering. This event is hosted by the Social Development team.
properties.Address: Old Nemaska

properties.Affiliation: Cree Nation of Nemaska, Social Development Department
properties.Title: Nemaska Trappers’ Festival
properties.Description: The Trappers’ Festival includes a demonstration of traditional teachings from our knowledge keepers, such as traditional tool making, sewing, net making and cooking. The Elders played a crucial role in preserving and passing down our cultural heritage and traditional skills. By highlighting these traditional teachings, the Trappers’ Festival aims to promote cultural education that has been passed on to generations. This winter event is always a tremendous success with a high degree of Community engagement.

properties.Affiliation: Cree Nation of Nemaska, Youth Council / Youth Department
properties.Title: Gas Subsidies for Cultural Excursions
properties.Description: Run by Youth Development, gas subsidies are provided to youth interested in participating in land-based activities such as hunting, fishing, and trapping. These subsidies cover the cost of gas and oil for their skidoos or vehicles.
properties.Address: 8 Lakeshore Road, Némiscau, QC, Canada, Quebec

properties.Affiliation: Cree Nation of Nemaska, Youth Council / Youth Department
properties.Title: Goose Break Bundles for Youth and Young Families
properties.Description: Run by the Nemaska Youth Council and Mammuu, the Goose Break Family Subsidy provides food and gas vouchers to promote and assist families in engaging with the outdoors and traditional lands during goose break. The age range for the youth bundles is for youth aged 13-17, and for the young families bundles is 18-35.
properties.Address: 8 Lakeshore Road, Némiscau, QC, Canada, Quebec

properties.Initiative_Type: Access to the Land, Knowledge & Infrastructure Development
properties.Title: Cabin Reconstruction Initiative
properties.Description: The 2023 Forest fires burnt 11% of Eeyou Istchee and affected nearly 60% of traplines, damaging 142 cabins. The Cree Trappers Association is overseeing funds to rebuild cabins, with 50 being built so far. This project will continue in 2026.
properties.Address: 4 Beaver Trail, Nemaska, QC, Canada, J0Y 3B0

properties.Title: Access to Traditional Food Program
properties.Description: Local CTA administrators hire local harvesters to provide traditional foods for elders, single-parent and low-income families who may not have access to a hunter or have limited financial resources for harvesting. It focuses mainly on harvesting fish, small game, and waterfowl. This initiative has been in place since the COVID-19 pandemic.

properties.Title: Community Freezer
properties.Description: The Cree Trappers Association (CTA) is installing community freezers to preserve and share traditional food with community members. Foods will vary according to seasonal availability, and may include moose, caribou, bear, beaver, goose, ptarmigan, fish, and berries.
properties.Address: 4 Beaver Trail, Nemaska, QC, Canada, J0Y 3B0

properties.Initiative_Type: Direct Access, Education and Culture, Production & Harvest, Knowledge & Infrastructure Development
properties.Title: Community Garden
properties.Description: This garden is composed of a dozen raised bed platforms used to grow vegetables and fruit trees. The fresh produce is shared with families, children and Elders, thus bringing healthy food to the community.  The community garden is a step toward healthier lifestyles and a stronger, more self-sustaining community. This initiative is run by the Social Development Department.
properties.Address: Rue Opemiska Meskino, Oujé-Bougoumou, QC, Canada, G0W 3C0

properties.Title: Blueberry farm
properties.Description: This is a 35-hectare commercial blueberry farm which is exploited by Staakun Enterprises. The harvest is mostly exported to Lac-Saint-Jean, but also includes blueberry-picking activities with elders and youth over the summer months.
properties.Address: Staakun Enterprises, 207 Rue Opemiska Meskino, Oujé-Bougoumou, QC G0W 3C0

properties.Title: Emergency Food Baskets
properties.Description: Emergency food baskets provide a three-day food supply for pregnant and post-partum women, offered twice per year at the CMC during emergencies.
properties.Address: 68 Opataca, Oujé-Bougoumou, QC, Canada, G0W 3C0

properties.Title: Meals on Wheels
properties.Description: Meals on Wheels are provided to CHB home care clientele, including elderly clients who cannot attend Multi-Service Day Centres (MSDC) programs.
properties.Address: 3 Wastawshkootaw Meskino, Oujé-Bougoumou, QC, Canada, G0W 3C0

properties.Title: Welcome Baby Food Basket
properties.Description: A food basket is delivered to new mothers after giving birth.
properties.Contact: Contact your local Awash nurse, PCCR, or nutritionist.
properties.Address: 68 Opataca, Oujé-Bougoumou, QC, Canada, G0W 3C0

properties.Title: ᒥᔫᐊᔑᒥᔓᐦ Miyuu-Ashimishuh
properties.Description: The Miyû-Ashimishuh program offers weekly coupons to pregnant mothers for healthy food until two months post-delivery. Participants can get weekly grocery coupons for nutritious foods such as milk, eggs, fruits and vegetables.
properties.Contact: Contact your local Awash nurse, PCCR, or nutritionist.
properties.Address: 68 Opataca, Oujé-Bougoumou, QC, Canada, G0W 3C0

properties.Title: Nutritional Counselling
properties.Description: Nutrition counselling can take place in an individual session or in a group/workshop setting (such as a cooking or baby-food-making class). To meet with a nutritionist, get an appointment at the CMC with a nurse or doctor, and discuss your nutritional needs with them. You can also call the MSDC and ask to speak with the nutritionist there.
properties.Address: 68 Opataca, Oujé-Bougoumou, QC, Canada, G0W 3C0

properties.Title: Breakfast Club Program
properties.Description: Elementary & high school students are provided breakfast through a partnership between Breakfast Club of Canada and regional entities (Cree School Board, Cree Nation Government, the Cree Health Board).
properties.Address: 200 Opemiska Street, Oujé-Bougoumou, QC, Canada, G0W 3C0

properties.Title: Children's Healthy Eating Program
properties.Description: This program runs after school and provides youth with healthy, nutritious snacks.
properties.Address: Wellness center, 19 Rue Opataca Meskino

properties.Title: Food Bank from the Food Assistance Program
properties.Description: The Food Assistance Program provides basic food products to those in need. Participants can access the food bank three times every four months at the Wellness Center. Non-perishables are also collected by donation, and through events such as a holiday food drive. This program targets low-income households, elders, families with children, people with disabilities and low-income individuals, as well as emergency cases.
properties.Address: Wellness center, 19 Rue Opataca Meskino

properties.Title: Soup Kitchen
properties.Description: The wellness center operates a soup kitchen, where tasty and healthy food is served to those in need. This initiative is financed by the Food Assistance Program.
properties.Address: Wellness center, 19 Rue Opataca Meskino

properties.Title: Healthy Baby Program
properties.Description: The goal of these events is to share recipes and prepare healthy snacks for mothers and babies.
properties.Address: Wellness center, 19 Rue Opataca Meskino

properties.Affiliation: Ouje-Bougoumou Cree Nation, Youth Council / Youth Department
properties.Title: Taco Tuesday
properties.Description: Every second Tuesday during the school year, the youth council organizes a free lunch for youth in the community, serving tacos, spaghetti, and occasionally traditional food. Cree Health Board representatives often participate and give info sessions covering a variety of health topics.
properties.Contact: Aliyah Stevens Bosum, Deputy Youth Chief
properties.Address: 110 Rue Oujé-Bougoumou Meskino, Oujé-Bougoumou, QC, Canada, G0W 3C0

properties.Title: Service of Traditional Food in Cree Health Board Food Services
properties.Description: Traditional food may be served in the Cree Health Board food services (elders' homes, MSDCs, Hospital), when available. Traditional food is provided by Cree Hunters who received training on the CHB traditional food program, including food safety and hygiene.
properties.Contact: Chantal Vinet-Lanouette, Program Officer (Nutrition), Regional Public Health Department
properties.Address: 68 Opataca, Oujé-Bougoumou, QC, Canada, G0W 3C0

properties.Title: Economic Security Program for Cree Hunters
properties.Description: The Economic Security Program for Cree Hunters is a family program which provides an annual income to Crees who have chosen hunting, trapping and fishing activities as a way of life. The program encourages the Crees to pursue harvesting activities by providing them with an income guaranteed, benefits and other incentives.
properties.Address: 207 Opemiska Street, Oujé-Bougoumou, QC, Canada, G0W 3C0

properties.Description: Biannual fishing excursion to Caniapiscau, to bring back a harvest for the community. This excursion highlights the strength and endurance of our men as we come together to provide for our people.

properties.Title: Cultural Village Initiatives
properties.Description: The Cultural Department of the band office offers a set of activities at the cultural village, including many food-related training sessions and workshops on topics such as moose hide preparation, beaver trapping, and sturgeon smoking.
properties.Address: Cultural Village

properties.Title: Pipuun Eetouin Winter Festival
properties.Description: Weeklong winter festival celebrating Cree culture. Activities include a fishing excursion using a net, fish preparation, moosehide preparation, and a feast.
properties.Address: Cultural Village

properties.Title: Chiiwedow Festival
properties.Description: Weeklong summer festival celebrating Cree culture. There are cultural activities, traditional teachings, and hands-on workshops for all ages. Activities include preparing moose hide and creating fish nets.
properties.Address: Ouje's point

properties.Title: Meuskum Eetowin Goose Festival
properties.Description: Spring festival with many activities related to goose hunting such as celebrating the first ever goose kills, a goose calling contest, and a community feast.
properties.Address: Cultural Village

properties.Initiative_Type: Access to the Land, Education and Culture
properties.Affiliation: Ouje-Bougoumou Cree Nation, Social Development Department
properties.Title: Land-Based Camp
properties.Description: This program promotes land-based education and connects community members to the land through traditional practices such as hunting, fishing, foraging, and medicinal plant gathering. A new land-based camp is being built on an island in Lake Opataca, about a 45-minute drive from Ouje-Bougoumou. The camp will host diverse programs for all ages, from family-oriented events that revitalize Cree language and culture to healing retreats for those struggling with drug and alcohol addiction.
properties.Contact: Brenda St. Pierre, Land Base Camp Project Manager, Youth Development Coordinator
properties.Address: Lake Opataca
properties.Phone:

properties.Description: Run by the Ouje-Bougoumou Youth Council and Maamuu Uphichinaausuutauu, the Goose Break Bundles for Young Families provides food and gas vouchers to promote and assist families in engaging with the outdoors and traditional lands during goose break. The age range for the youth bundles is for youth aged 13-17, and for the young families bundles is 18-35.

properties.Address: 110 Rue Oujé-Bougoumou Meskino, Oujé-Bougoumou, QC, Canada, G0W 3C0

properties.Affiliation: Ouje-Bougoumou Cree Nation, Youth Council / Youth Department
properties.Title: Youth & Elders Program
properties.Description: The Youth & Elders Program offers a range of cultural activities bringing together youth and elders, such as hunting and traditional cookout outs on the land. This includes moose and bear hunting in the fall, ptarmigan in the winter and fishing in the summer.
properties.Contact: Aliyah Stevens Bosum, Deputy Youth Chief
properties.Address: 110 Rue Oujé-Bougoumou Meskino, Oujé-Bougoumou, QC, Canada, G0W 3C0

properties.Title: Eeyou Istchee Healthy Food Network
properties.Description: This network is for businesses & organizations involved in the food sector in Eeyou Istchee. It aims to improve availability, quality and price of healthy foods by sharing information relevant to the food sector,  such as programs, resources, training and funding opportunities available in our region.
properties.Address: Eeyou Istchee

properties.Title: SAES Professional Cooking Training
properties.Description: Professional cooking training is offered by Sabtuan Adult Education to get the skills and experience needed to become a professional chef.
properties.Address: 1 Elder David Neeposh Street, Waswanipi, Qc, Canada, J0Y 3C0

properties.Initiative_Type: Education and Culture, Knowledge & Infrastructure Development
properties.Title: Safe food handling training program for traditional and store-bought foods
properties.Description: Safe food handling trainings are offered to ensure that traditional and store-bought foods are of the best quality when served at community events, family gatherings and institutional settings like the Chisasibi hospital, MSDCs, Group Homes, Reception Center, Childcare centers and Elder Homes.
properties.Contact: Lilian Kandiliotis and Chantal Vinet-Lanouette, Public Health Nutritionists
properties.Address: Eeyou Istchee

properties.Title: Improving access to drinking water in Eeyou Istchee
properties.Description: Led by the CNG-CBHSSJB Joint Committee on Access to Nutritious Food in Eeyou Istchee, this project involves installing new water fountains, as well as fixing or upgrading existing fountains. For the first phase of this project, we are targeting public buildings such as the sports centers, the swimming pools, the arenas, the youth centers, etc. Other public buildings, such as daycares and schools, could also be invited to participate in the future.
properties.Address: Eeyou Istchee

properties.Affiliation: Cree Health Board (CBHSSJB), Cree Nation Government, Cree Trappers Association (CTA)
properties.Title:
Working Committee
on Access to Nutritious Food
properties.Description: This committee has been active since 2015, working to strengthen collaboration between regional entities to ensure affordable, nutritious food for all, promote healthy food choices, and develop adequate management practices of the food system.
properties.Contact: Chantal Vinet-Lanouette, Public Health Nutritionist and Catherine Godin, Planning, Programming and Research Officer
properties.Address: Eeyou Istchee

properties.Affiliation: Cree Nation Government's Justice and Correctional Services
properties.Title: Traditional Food While in Detention
properties.Description: The traditional food program brings Elders into detention facilities to cook traditional meals with Cree inmates and to spend time speaking with them about Cree values and the steps forward. The selection of foods varies with the seasons, aligning with the hunting periods. This program is currently offered in certain detention facilities only, with the objective of extending it as widely as possible.
properties.Address: 301 Queen Street, Mistissini, QC, Canada, G0W 1C0
properties.Links: Webpage on the Traditional Food program: https://creejustice.ca/index.php/ca/programs/corrections-and-reintegration-work/traditional-food-detention-program

properties.Initiative_Type: Education and Culture, Knowledge & Infrastructure Development
properties.Title: Eeyou Ituun Training
properties.Description: The Cree Land Stewardship (Eeyou/Eenou Ituun) Training Program is a fully accredited 900-hour collegial program that leads to an official Attestation of Collegial Studies (ACS) certified by the Québec Ministry of Education. The program is offered in partnership between the Cree Trappers Association and the Cégep de St-Félicien. The Eeyou ltuun, Cree Land Stewardship Program is designed to teach Cree who are not experienced land users the fundamental skills to be effective Cree Land Stewards.
properties.Contact: Judy Coon-Come, Eeyou Eenou Ituun Coordinator/ Communication Officer
properties.Address: 1105 Bd Hamel, Saint-Félicien, QC, Canada, G8K 2R8

CTA Website: https://creetrappers.ca/special-project/eeyou-ituun-training-program

properties.Title: Wildlife Disease Testing
properties.Description: The Cree Trappers' Association tests abnormal harvested game for diseases to track disease spread, new diseases and to help hunters know if their meat is safe to eat. If you have harvested a sick animal, help keep our land and food safe by reporting your findings.
properties.Address: Eeyou Istchee

properties.Title: Cree Geo-Portal
properties.Description: The Cree Geoportal is a Mapping Resources for Eeyou Istchee. Requiring an account to access most data, it provides information on wildlife harvest & monitoring, fish consumption recommendations per trapline, Niskamoon funded initiatives, and climate change observations.

properties.Affiliation: Cree Trappers Association (CTA), Concordia University, Laval University
properties.Title: FISHES: Fostering Indigenous Small-scale fisheries for Health, Economy & Food Security
properties.Description: This Multi-year project from 2019-2025 explores the genetics of important species of fish in Eeyou Istchee, Nunavik and Nunavut, namely Cisco, Whitefish, and Brook Trout. The project focuses on identifying genetically distinct fish populations which will help maintain sustainable harvesting practices, and identify significant fisheries within the Eeyou Marine Region (EMR) for future protection.
properties.Contact: Louis Bernatchez (Laval University), Monica Mulrennan (Concordia University), Dylan Fraser (Concordia University), Natasha Louttit (CTA-EMR)

properties.Affiliation: Cree Trappers Association (CTA), Cree Nation Government, Cree Health Board (CBHSSJB)

properties.Description: This project aims to support local harvesting, packaging, transformation and distribution of blueberries. It is a collaboration among the Cree Trappers Association, the Cree Health Board, the Cree Nation Government, and the Department of Social Development. This initiative varies year to year, depending on funding, with a goal to make it a stable annual initiative.
properties.Contact: Rodney Mark, Director of Social and Cultural Development, CNG, Chantal Vinet-Lanouette, Public Health Nutritionist, CBHSSJB, and Thomas Stevens, Special Projects Coordinator, CTA
properties.Address: Eeyou Istchee

properties.Title: Community-based Wildlife and Climate Change Monitoring
properties.Description: This initiative encourages residents to share their knowledge and report observations of land and wildlife. Residents can report sightings of polar bears, tracks or signs (e.g., denning), belugas, shorebirds, waterfowl, nesting colonies, other bird species, invasive species, new or unusual species, and harvest data.

properties.Affiliation: Eeyou Marine Region Wildlife Board (EMRWB), Niskamoon
properties.Title: Eeyou Coastal Habitat Comprehensive Research Project
properties.Description: The decline of eelgrass has coincided with larger coastal changes, including a drop in the quality of spring and fall goose hunts by Cree land users. The Coastal Habitat Comprehensive Research Project seeks to understand the link between the different factors of this complex system. Started in 2016, this is a Cree-driven project that involves four Cree coastal communities: Waskaganish, Eastmain, Wemindji and Chisasibi.

properties.Title: Caribou Ungava Research Project
properties.Description: Caribou Ungava is a large research program to assess the impacts of climate change and industrial activities on the two major migratory caribou herds of northern Quebec and Labrador. Ongoing since 2009, the project is now it it's third phase.

properties.Title: Niimaawin: Food for Travel
properties.Description: Initiated in 2023, this collaboration between COTA and Pastilles is working to develop “Niimaawin” (the Cree word for “food for travel”), which will provide handy and nutritious solutions inspired by Cree culinary culture and prepared using boreal herbs and spices. These solutions include food products and meals designed for adventures in the bush and on the water, as well as during winter excursions.
properties.Contact: Robin McGinley, COTA Executive Director, and Emmanuelle Choquette, Associate at Papilles Développement

properties.Description: The Cree Trappers Association (CTA) is installing community freezers to preserve and share traditional food with community members. Foods will vary according to seasonal availability, and may include moose, caribou, bear, beaver, goose, ptarmigan, fish, and berries.
properties.Address: 57 Rte de Waskaganish, Waskaganish, QC, Canada, J0M 1R0

properties.Initiative_Type: Direct Access, Education and Culture, Production & Harvest
properties.Title: Smokey Hill Gathering - Fall Fishery Program
properties.Description: Cisco is harvested from the Rupert, Nottaway and Broadback rivers over the months of September and October. All fish are cleaned, smoked and supplied to community members. These fish are available to the public for consumption at no cost, as this is a paid training activity. The Smokey Hill gathering is held every second week of September, and it is a week of teachings, games, and entertainment, all held at Smokey Hill landing during the Cisco run.
properties.Contact: Stacy Bear, Manager, Culture & Tourism Programs and Jack Diamond, Siibii Director & President
properties.Address: 70 Waskaganish Street, Waskaganish, QC, Canada, J0M1R1

properties.Initiative_Type: Direct Access, Education and Culture, Production & Harvest
properties.Title: Summer Fishery Program
properties.Description: Fish is harvested from the Rupert, Nottaway and Broadback rivers over July and August. Species include pike, sturgeon, whitefish and walleye. All fish are cleaned, smoked and supplied to community members while the fish are prepared as plates for local distribution.
properties.Address: 70 Waskaganish Street, Waskaganish, QC, Canada, J0M1R1

properties.Initiative_Type: Access to the Land, Education and Culture
properties.Title: Wiichihiiwewin Land-Based Programs
properties.Description: The Wiichihiiwewin Centre organizes a multitude of land-based programs to teach life skills and promote healthy living. These include a wilderness camp for youth, healing journeys and Wiichihiisuu land-based. Under the guidance of elders, these programs bring participants to develop skills such as skinning beavers, working with a moose hide, fishing, rabbit snaring, beaver trapping, beading and sewing.
properties.Address: 18 Rue Nottaway, Waskaganish, QC, Canada, J0M 1R0
properties.Links:

properties.Affiliation: Cree Nation of Waskaganish, Cultural Department
properties.Title: Elders Lunch Program
properties.Description: The lunch program is available starting in December and runs throughout the winter. It is run by the Cultural Department and serves both traditional and store-bought foods.
properties.Contact: Stacy Bear, Manager, Culture & Tourism Programs
properties.Address: 70 Waskaganish Street, Waskaganish, QC, Canada, J0M1R1

properties.Title: Wiichihiiwewin Cooking Workshops
properties.Description: Monthly cooking with moms and cooking with the guys workshops are livestreamed on the Wiichihiiwewin Centre's Facebook page. This program lasts for 1h in the early evening and includes both cooking of store-bought foods and traditional foods.
properties.Address: 18 Rue Nottaway, Waskaganish, QC, Canada, J0M 1R0

properties.Title: Waswanipi Community Garden
properties.Description: This garden will provide fresh fruits, vegetables, a space for learning, and a place where our community can come together. properties.Address: 3 West Aspen, Waswanipi, QC, Canada, J0Y 3C0

properties.Affiliation: Cree Nation of Waswanipi, Wellness Department
properties.Title: Waswanipi Lunch Program
properties.Description: Our Lunch Program is providing and supporting busy working parents, providing delicious meals for children so they can concentrate and pay attention more at school. properties.Title: Greenhouse
properties.Description: Greenhouse projects have been in the works since 2021, with a first growing season in 2022. A larger pilot project took place in 2024 and was successful, leading to the construction of a 42-foot growing dome and the online training of two greenhouse technicians with the distributor Green Iglu. They were able to harvest and distribute fresh produce in September and October. Heating has been added to the greenhouse to make it a 4-season and they are looking to build a new A-frame greenhouse in 2025.

properties.Affiliation: Cree Nation of Wemindji, Odonaterra Community Environmental Strategies
properties.Title: Wemindji Food Resilience Planning
properties.Description: In collaboration with the consulting firm Odonaterra, the Cree Nation of Wemindji is strengthening local food security by combining traditional knowledge, local food production, and community engagement. A community working group created a detailed food system map that identified over 40 food access points, such as grocery stores, gardens, traditional harvesting support, food education, and local food production. This allowed the community to identify their vulnerabilities, strengths, and priorities, from which they are currently developing an action plan.

properties.Address: Whapmagoostui, QC, Canada, J0M 1G0

Whapmagoostui Hunter & Trapper Facebook group (private): https://www.facebook.com/groups/1324448048332244/ ;

properties.Title: Community Freezer
properties.Description: The Cree Trappers Association (CTA) is installing community freezers to preserve and share traditional food with community members. Foods will vary according to seasonal availability, and may include moose, caribou, bear, beaver, goose, ptarmigan, fish, and berries.
properties.Address:
PO Box 450
Whapmagoostui, QC  J0M 1G0

properties.Title: Soup Kitchen
properties.Description: Traditional foods such as bannock, ptarmigan, and caribou are served at the cultural camp soup kitchen Lodge. Open to anyone.

properties.Title: Traditional Food Cooking Workshops
properties.Description: Workshops for youth on preparing and cooking tasty meals with caribou, ptarmigans, beaver, fish, etc.
properties.Address: Cultural camp Kitchen Lodge

properties.Title: Traditional Skills workshops
properties.Description: Workshops on a variety of key traditional skills, such as how to clean and prepare bones, how to set and check beaver traps, how to properly clean caribou heads, and how to prepare traditional medicine. Workshops and teachings depend on the season.
properties.Address: Cultural camp

properties.Affiliation: Whapmagoostui First Nation, Youth Council / Youth Department
properties.Title: Goose Break Bundles for Youth and Young Families
properties.Description: Run by the Whapmagoostui Youth Department, the Maamuu Uhpichinaausuutaau Goose Break Grocery and Supplies Coupons assist families in engaging with the outdoors and traditional lands during goose break. The age range for the youth bundles is for youth aged 13-17, and for the young families bundles is 18-35.
properties.Address: 415 Whapmakw Meskino, Whapmagoostui, QC, Canada, J0M 1G0
